meaning: n.养育,教育,教养,营养品vt.养育,给与营养物,教养
examples: Parents want to know the best way to nurture and raise their child to adulthood.
Unique to mammals, the limbic cortex impels creatures to nurture their offspring by delivering feelings of tenderness and warmth to the parent when children are nearby.
It's important to nurture a good working relationship.
"Mike knows how to nurture design, " says Goeman, whose unit oversaw the development of Resolve.
Difference calls for an adaptation of parental expectation, challenging traditional ideas about nurture and support.
